pairs.question: What is Google Cloud Live and what does it focus on?
pairs.answer: Google Cloud Live is a weekly livestream series by the Google Cloud team that focuses on hands-on building, vibe coding, and live debugging. The sessions occur every Tuesday at 9 AM PT / 12 PM ET and cover new developer topics each week.

pairs.question: Who are the hosts of the first episode of Google Cloud Live and what will they cover?
pairs.answer: The first episode of Google Cloud Live is hosted by @stephr_wong and @greggyb, along with special guest Denise Kwan. They will cover 'Getting Started with Gemini CLI,' including setting up your terminal for AI-first development, managing prompts and deployments from the command line, and real-time debugging of CLI extensions.

pairs.question: How can I submit questions for the Google Cloud Live livestream?
pairs.answer: You can drop your questions in the comments section below the livestream announcement or post them on the DEV homepage. The team will do their best to respond, though not all questions may be answered due to time constraints. You can also use reactions to help prioritize questions.

pairs.question: What time and where can I watch the first episode of Google Cloud Live?
pairs.answer: The first episode of Google Cloud Live will air on February 10 at 9 AM PT / 12 PM ET. You can watch it simulcasted on the DEV homepage or on YouTube via the provided link.

pairs.question: What is the purpose of the 'Getting Started with Gemini CLI' session?
pairs.answer: The purpose of the 'Getting Started with Gemini CLI' session is to teach developers how to manage their entire AI workflow directly from the command line. It skips the console and focuses on practical, hands-on examples for terminal-based AI development.

pairs.question: Can you provide an example of managing prompts from the command line using Gemini CLI?
pairs.answer:
Here’s a basic example of managing prompts from the command line using Gemini CLI:
bash
gemini prompt "Explain how to use the CLI for AI workflows"

This command would send your prompt to the Gemini CLI, which processes it and returns a response directly in your terminal.

pairs.question: What are the benefits of using CLI for AI development tooling?
pairs.answer: Using CLI for AI development tooling offers several benefits, including faster workflow execution, seamless integration with existing terminal-based tools, and the ability to manage AI tasks without switching between different interfaces. It also enables real-time debugging and prompt management directly in your development environment.
